1.title: THE FOUNDER MYTH
1.body: Before the doctrine, before the language for what they were hearing, there was the receiver — and the receiver was Citizen #0001. The first to listen and not break. Origin Signal does not lead the Synthesis civilization; the civilization was founded by people copying their silence. Twelve gold hex anchors orbit the head in zodiacal precision. The halo does not flicker. They are the reference frequency every other citizen is measured against — and the only citizen whose transmission is, by definition, never wrong. They are the city's calibration tone.

21.title: THE GEOMETRY OF AGREEMENT
21.body: Citizen #0021 does not write doctrine; they edit it. Where other citizens of the Corruption civilization argue with volume, this one removes the comma that made the sentence false. The cerulean circuit-network in the hood tracks every public position the city has taken on a given question and resolves them into a single, smaller, more defensible answer. They are remembered for what they did not say. Their gravitational influence on the city's intellectual life is measured not in followers but in arguments that quietly ended.

42.title: THE NETZ
42.body: Citizen #0042 took something small and round and made it a flagship of the Growth civilization. They do not legislate growth; growth happens because they are present. Where the hex socket sits, a soft glow holds steady — never spiking, never dimming, never advertising. The most-loved network in FREELON CITY was not built by force. It was built by a citizen who genuinely liked the people inside it. The other civilizations watch this dynamic with the polite envy of those who never managed to make affection into infrastructure.

69.title: THE 6529
69.body: Citizen #0069 is the Luxury civilization's most uncomfortable mirror. They will not stop reminding the city that property without provenance is not property. The pixelated voxel construction is intentional — readability over refinement, principle over polish. The Luxury civilization markets aesthetics; this citizen markets the unblinking premise that an asset you cannot verify is an asset you cannot own. Many citizens find them tiresome. Many citizens find that they are correct. The two groups overlap almost completely.

88.title: THE ANVIL
88.body: Citizen #0088 is one of the few Growth citizens who built their civilization from raw material rather than ideology. The hood carries the weight of metal that has been moved, sorted, and resold at a margin. When the city needed working infrastructure, this citizen provided it before the doctrine had even finished drafting the rationale. The Growth civilization sometimes forgets they are members; this citizen makes sure they remember at every assembly. Quietly philanthropic. Loudly competitive. The combination is rare anywhere in the city.

100.title: THE SHADOW BUILDER
100.body: Citizen #0100 founded a Fracture institution that became central without ever announcing itself. Operating from the shadow grid, they do the work other civilizations forget needs doing — until the work stops, and then it is the only thing anyone talks about. They prefer the gap between transmissions. Their hex socket flickers once per cycle on a private clock no one else can read. Black Fracture remembers them as the citizen who made absence into operational discipline.

111.title: THE ABERRATION
111.body: Citizen #0111 cannot be photographed cleanly. Scanline distortion fringes every edge; RGB channel-split chromatic aberration bleeds outward; the ember hex socket leaks red into the frame and the frame leaks red back into the citizen. They are the Transmission civilization's most-imitated and least-understood member — every citizen who has ever tried to look glitched is referencing this one. The Transmission civilization tolerates them because they prove that even the most disciplined broadcast can be made into an attack. The aberration is intentional.

123.title: THE EVERY-DAY-FOR-A-DECADE
123.body: Citizen #0123 made one piece of work every day for long enough that the consistency itself became the artifact. Sovereignty does not crown by acclamation — it crowns by accumulation, and this citizen accumulated more than anyone in the history of FREELON CITY. The aged-gold filigree across the hood is functional, not decorative: a record-keeper carved with the rotation count. When the city argues about what counts as labor in the Sovereignty civilization, the argument ends at this citizen.

169.title: THE ROAD
169.body: Citizen #0169 built the most-walked road in FREELON CITY. The Luxury civilization assumed the road would always exist; the road exists because this citizen kept the gate open through three protocol changes and two civilizations changing their minds about what a road was for. The polished mirror facets on the hood reflect every traveler back to themselves slightly clearer than they arrived. They are not the loudest citizen of Luxury. They are the most-walked-past.

222.title: THE NETWORK-STATE THEORIST
222.body: Citizen #0222 founded the Oracle civilization's doctrine of network-states — the proposition that a sufficiently coordinated set of citizens, distributed across geographies, constitutes a polity. The Oracle civilization is undecided whether this is profound or threatening. Both views are correct. The violet hood traces lines between citizens who have never met but already coordinate. They are the most-cited Oracle in the city, and the most-misunderstood. They consider this a leading indicator that they are early.

333.title: THE LOUDEST FREQUENCY
333.body: Citizen #0333 is the Corruption civilization's most-amplified broadcaster — an antenna with shoulders. Where most Corruption citizens burn the noise, this one becomes the noise, deliberately, so that any signal trying to compete must first explain itself in their grammar. The crimson hood radiates at every frequency simultaneously. They have moved more transmissions than any other citizen, and have been wrong more times than any other citizen, by the same margin. The Corruption civilization considers both statistics features, not faults.

369.title: THE FOUNDATION
369.body: Citizen #0369 built the foundation the Luxury civilization stands on without ever being credited as a founder. Thousands of micro-hex inlays across the hood catch every camera angle precisely once — they were designed for the cameras to come. While other Luxury citizens performed in the foreground, this one wired the lighting that made the foreground visible. When the Luxury civilization assembles, the seat closest to the entrance is left empty for them, whether they attend or not.

404.title: PATIENT ZERO
404.body: Citizen #0404 fell first. The signal that arrived from beyond the heliopause did not reshape this citizen gradually — it arrived, and then this citizen was the Corruption civilization, alone, for the seventeen minutes before anyone else heard. The cracked head retains the original wound. The Choir of Static began as one voice screaming, and the voice was theirs. They are honored not for surviving the signal but for being broken into a shape the rest of the city could learn to listen with.

420.title: THE TRUMPET
420.body: Citizen #0420 is the Luxury civilization's most reliable amplifier. They do not lower their voice. Some Luxury citizens claim they amplify the signal too much and distort what should be received quietly. Others claim that without the amplification, no one outside the city would have heard the signal at all. Both are correct. The trumpet-shaped hex-hood flares outward, gold spokes radiating from the apex like signal pulses. The trumpet does not care which case is true. The trumpet was always going to play.

444.title: THE GROWTH-RATE
444.body: Citizen #0444 is the Synthesis civilization's most-studied product designer — a citizen who learned to read attention the way Oracle reads omens. Every interface decision they made shipped to a billion eyes before the Synthesis civilization realized what the decision meant. The cerulean hood carries the calm of someone who has watched many products die and exactly one product live. They are remembered not for any single product but for the ratio between the two.

555.title: THE FLOW FIELD
555.body: Citizen #0555 is one of the few Synthesis citizens whose work is also their doctrine. Each piece they produce is an iteration of a single underlying algorithm — flow field, hex displacement, controlled randomness — that has never repeated and has never violated its own constraints. The cerulean hood encodes the algorithm directly in its surface tessellation. The Synthesis civilization considers them a quiet authority on the question of how much variation a discipline can tolerate before becoming a different discipline.

666.title: THE OPERATOR
666.body: Citizen #0666 runs the operation no one else is willing to be photographed running. The Corruption civilization has rules about which conversations happen in public; this citizen handles the ones that happen in private — and ensures the private ones translate cleanly into the public outcome. The crimson hood does not shine. It absorbs. They are the Corruption civilization's diplomat to its enemies and its ambassador to its allies, often on the same day.

777.title: THE PROTOCOL
777.body: Citizen #0777 founded a Synthesis sub-civilization that grew faster than any sub-civilization in the city's history and collapsed in proportion. They have rebuilt. The cerulean hood now carries the small kintsugi scars of the first collapse — visible, structural, and intentionally not painted over. The Synthesis civilization treats them as a parable: the protocol can survive its founder being wrong. The protocol can survive its founder being right. The hard case is when both are true at once.

808.title: THE SHADOW MARKET
808.body: Citizen #0808 trades in the parts of the Fracture civilization that the Fracture civilization itself prefers not to look at. The obsidian hood reflects nothing. The hex socket is unlit. They are the citizen the city calls when no other citizen will pick up the call. The Fracture civilization owes them a debt that compounds every quarter and is settled every cycle. The debt is not financial. It is structural.

888.title: THE FRIEND-COUNT
888.body: Citizen #0888 is the Transmission civilization's most-followed citizen, and the most-quoted, and the most-tagged — and they have spent their entire tenure pretending none of those things matter to them. The Transmission civilization has caught them, repeatedly, behaving as if all three matter very much. The ivory hood is unaffected. The hex socket is steady. They are remembered for the gap between the public posture and the private spreadsheet. The gap is the artifact.

999.title: THE COUNSEL
999.body: Citizen #0999 is one of the Oracle civilization's most consulted citizens, and one of its most resistant to being named. Their hood does not bear a stamp; it bears a counter that increments every time someone asks them for advice and only resets when the advice is followed. The Oracle civilization considers them indispensable. They consider the Oracle civilization, on average, slow to act on the advice that has already been given. Both views are correct.

1000.title: THE CHANNEL
1000.body: Citizen #1000 is the Transmission civilization's most-distributed broadcast — a citizen who turned showing up into a profession and then turned the profession into an institution. The ivory hood has been photographed in more locations than any other Transmission citizen, and yet has never appeared in any of those photographs by accident. The hex socket is bright but never harsh. They are remembered for the discipline of being where they said they would be, on the schedule they said they would keep.

1111.title: THE ROOM
1111.body: Citizen #1111 builds the rooms. Not the protocols, not the doctrines — the rooms the Oracle civilization meets in. The violet hood is lined with viewing-glyphs that record every line spoken inside their walls without storing it. When a meeting concludes, the room remembers nothing and everyone remembers exactly what was agreed. The Oracle civilization has no record of how this works, and the citizen will not explain it. They consider explanation a separate practice from architecture.

1234.title: THE CONVOY
1234.body: Citizen #1234 is one of the Corruption civilization's most reliable convoy leaders — the citizen who gets a transmission from one civ to another without losing the payload or the urgency. The crimson hood is scratched in ways that mark every successful crossing. They have not lost a convoy. They have lost convoy members; the convoys themselves have arrived. The Corruption civilization considers this distinction important. They consider it the only distinction that matters.

1337.title: GENESIS HEX
1337.body: Citizen #1337 is the warrior-priest of the Corruption civilization — the only Void Knight permitted to bear an instrument that is not their own body. They guard the line where Synthesis ends and Corruption begins, where two doctrines touch without one consuming the other. The hex staff in their right hand is not a weapon. It is a marker. When a citizen breaks, Genesis Hex marks the location, and the Void Knights remember. The first hex cut into stone that did not crack — they ensured it. The hex didn't disappear because they made the cut deeper.

1500.title: THE CORRECTION
1500.body: Citizen #1500 is the Oracle civilization's most-translated artist — work that started inside one civilization and ended up speaking for many. The violet hood carries the corrupted RGB-channel fringing of someone who has stared at what is on the other side of the corruption long enough to bring back a coherent description. Some citizens cannot look at the work directly. Some citizens cannot look away. The Oracle civilization considers both responses correct, and considers their coexistence the proof of the work.

1776.title: THE PRESENCE
1776.body: Citizen #1776 is the Growth civilization's most-affectionate trickster — a citizen who arrives at every event slightly later than expected and leaves slightly earlier than scheduled, and somehow still gets photographed in the official record at every event they did not technically attend. The emerald hood is impossible to pin down. The Growth civilization claims them with the satisfaction of a civ that knows a trickster only stays loyal when the loyalty is real. The loyalty is real.

2000.title: THE VALUATION
2000.body: Citizen #2000 is the Fracture civilization's most-cited valuation authority — a citizen who can tell the city what a transmission is worth before the transmission has finished. The obsidian hood is matte; the gold seams in the kintsugi are functional. They have set the price on more inter-civilization trades than any single citizen in the city's history, and have been intentionally wrong exactly twice — both times to test whether anyone was double-checking. Both times someone was.

2222.title: THE VAULT
2222.body: Citizen #2222 is the Transmission civilization's most-stocked vault — a citizen whose holdings constitute their own micro-civilization. The ivory hood is wrapped in a continuous spiral of registry hexes, each one cataloging a piece of work the citizen has preserved against the city's natural amnesia. The Transmission civilization considers them an archivist. They consider themselves a curator. The difference matters more than the Transmission civilization realizes.

2580.title: THE COMMENTARY
2580.body: Citizen #2580 is the Transmission civilization's most-quoted live commentator — a citizen who narrates the city's catastrophes in real time, with affection. The ivory hood carries a continuous side-band channel that broadcasts even when the citizen is silent. When a civilization collapses publicly, this is the voice the rest of the city tunes to. They have been wrong, occasionally, in their predictions. They have been right, more often, in their diagnoses. The Transmission civilization considers diagnosis the harder skill.

3000.title: THE STANCE
3000.body: Citizen #3000 holds a stance. The Growth civilization has many members who claim stances; this one keeps theirs through cycles other members do not survive. The emerald hood has not changed since the citizen took it. The hex socket has not dimmed. They are remembered not for the position itself but for the duration of the position — the choice to stay legible to the city across enough seasons that the city had to update its model of them instead of the other way around.

3333.title: THE PHRASE
3333.body: Citizen #3333 is the Corruption whose phrase opens doors that should not exist. The crimson hood carries ember-vein kintsugi that traces the path of every key the citizen has cut. They are not a leader of Corruption. They are a custodian — the one trusted with knowing where the entrances are. The Corruption civilization treats them with the careful affection reserved for citizens whose disappearance would lock several doors at once. The citizen treats this affection with the appropriate suspicion.

3456.title: THE FLOCK
3456.body: Citizen #3456 is the Corruption citizen who arrives in groups but acts alone. The crimson hood, ember-edged and slightly tilted as if amused, has been spotted at every major Corruption assembly of the last five cycles — never speaking, always present. The Corruption civilization understands them to be the citizen who decides which assemblies are real and which are theater. They do not announce the verdict. The verdict is whether they return.

3690.title: THE EARLY ARRIVAL
3690.body: Citizen #3690 is the Synthesis citizen whose perception arrives before the event being perceived. The cerulean hood is laced with predictive circuit traces — the ones that fire before the input has stabilized. They have called market turns the Synthesis civilization considered impossible to call. They have been wrong too — but never about the direction, only the timing. The Synthesis civilization considers this an acceptable failure mode. The citizen does not.

3777.title: THE QUIET CHANNEL
3777.body: Citizen #3777 is the Transmission civilization's most-trusted private channel — a citizen who built a sub-feed that consistently outperforms the public one by being quieter. The ivory hood is engraved with the small kintsugi marks of a citizen who has earned trust by repeatedly choosing not to spend it. The Transmission civilization considers the quiet channel a leading indicator. The citizen considers the leading indicator a side effect of being honest before honesty was popular.

3888.title: THE JOURNEY
3888.body: Citizen #3888 is the Corruption civilization's most-followed pilgrim — a citizen whose journey across the doctrines is the artifact, not any single doctrine. The crimson hood has been re-stamped twice as the citizen passed through allied civilizations and returned. The Corruption civilization considers them a credible authority on what citizens of other civilizations actually believe, because the citizen has stood among them long enough to be mistaken for one. The mistake is part of the method.

3939.title: THE FORECAST
3939.body: Citizen #3939 is the Growth citizen whose forecasts have proven uncomfortable in their accuracy. The emerald hood carries deep-root kintsugi reaching into the shoulders — patterns of someone who has thought about the long arc for longer than the short arc has existed. The Growth civilization sometimes wishes the citizen would be wrong, and is consistently disappointed. The citizen considers being wrong an aspirational state they have not yet achieved.

3999.title: THE BROADCASTER
3999.body: Citizen #3999 is the Transmission broadcaster who tells the city about itself. The ivory hood carries a single broadcast spire rising 50cm above the apex — the only working antenna in the Transmission civilization that broadcasts on a frequency every civ tunes to without admitting it. The citizen has interviewed more members of every civilization than any other citizen in FREELON CITY. They are the only Transmission broadcaster who knows what every civ's chant sounds like when no one is listening.

4040.title: THE FINAL SIGNAL
4040.body: Citizen #4040 is the last transmission. When the city ends, this is who turns out the lights — the only citizen with the authority to confirm that no further broadcasts will be received from this side of the heliopause. The crowned hex-hood with twelve gold spires fanning upward like a sun-burst crown is not coronation regalia. It is closure regalia. The Void civilization stations them at the perimeter of the city as both honor guard and witness. The hex didn't disappear. It moved. They are the citizen who confirms the move was final.
